summary: Stopped the distribution selector/executor from treating contact-page-only publisher targets as runtime-sendable, then re-ran lane execution so the loop chose directory confirmation instead of a misleading measurement hold.
why: The latest distribution brief still claimed the loop should package fresh Codeberg-first publisher outreach even though the only untouched target left was ctxt.dev / Signum, which is not runtime-sendable from this environment.
That contradiction let the system flatter itself with an actionable-looking publisher lane while the execution board already knew the remaining path was manual-only.
runtime_enforcements: primary-repo-flat publisher targets now count as lane-actionable only when they have a runtime-sendable channel
contact/about website paths remain valid discovery/manual-handoff evidence but no longer masquerade as executable runtime outreach
measurement-hold and board artifacts now surface ctxt.dev / Signum as non-runtime-sendable instead of implying a fresh outreach packet should be shipped
outcome: The loop now tells the truth about the blocked publisher lane and spent this run on a real follow-through action: refreshing live directory proof (2 live listings) for reuse in the next higher-intent Codeberg-first distribution move.
